codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all 9 requirements from the feature description. Commander.js program is correctly configured with name, version, and all global options. API client has Bearer auth, error mapping, and HTTPS enforcement. API key resolution follows the specified priority chain. Config file management uses 0600 permissions. Output formatters support table/json/csv with TTY auto-detection. Exit codes are correct (0-3). Auth commands (login/logout/status/token) and config commands (get/set/list) work as specified. Unauthenticated errors show helpful messages. 96 tests provide thorough coverage across all modules. One notable gap: the feature description mentions 'retry' for the API client, but no retry/backoff logic is implemented.

issues.description: Feature description specifies 'retry' for the API client (HTTPS fetch with Bearer auth, error mapping, retry), but no retry or exponential backoff logic is implemented. Transient failures (network errors, 429, 5xx) are thrown immediately. This is a gap vs the spec but doesn't break core functionality — retry can be added incrementally.

colWidths is constructed with `.filter(Boolean)` which could produce an array shorter than the number of columns if only some columns have a `width` property. cli-table3 expects colWidths to match column count or be omitted. This will only cause issues if callers provide widths for some columns but not others.

The --no-color option is defined on the Commander program but never wired to chalk. Chalk v5 respects process.env.NO_COLOR, but Commander's --no-color flag only sets an internal option (program.opts().color = false) without setting the env var. The flag has no actual effect on chalk output.

bin entry points to './dist/index.ts' — a .ts extension in bin is unconventional. Works with bun as the runtime, but would fail if the package were built via tsc and consumed as standard Node. Acceptable for a private package run via bun.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly added 'test-cli: bun --filter @totus/cli test' command to .factory/services.yaml. Good practice.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml diff in commit af9fd06 — added test-cli command

sharedStateObservations.observation: The CLI package has no lint script in its package.json, and services.yaml only has lint for @totus/web. The backend-worker skill procedure says to run 'bun run lint' as part of verification, but there's no lint config for the CLI package. Worker correctly skipped lint (nothing to run), but this gap between the skill instruction and the actual CLI package setup could confuse future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/cli/package.json has no 'lint' script. .factory/services.yaml lint command only covers @totus/web. Skill step 5 says 'bun run lint — zero warnings'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The CLI uses chalk v5 (ESM-only, no color level config API) and cli-table3 for terminal output. These library choices and their import patterns (e.g., csv-stringify/sync for synchronous CSV generation) could be documented in library/architecture.md under a CLI Patterns section so future workers know which libraries to use.

summary: Feature cli-core passes review. The implementation comprehensively covers all 9 requirements: Commander.js setup with global options, shared API client with Bearer auth and error mapping, API key resolution priority chain, config file management with 0600 permissions, table/json/csv output formatters with TTY auto-detection, correct exit codes (0-3), auth commands (login/logout/status/token), config commands (get/set/list), and helpful unauthenticated error messages. 96 tests across 7 test files provide excellent coverage. Non-blocking issues: missing retry logic in API client (mentioned in spec), --no-color flag not wired to chalk, minor colWidths edge case in formatters, and .ts bin entry. Worker followed the skill procedure and produced a clean handoff.
